controlName: Establish and Maintain the Asset HAZOP Plan
controlStatement: Each Asset must establish and maintain a HAZOP plan (integrated within the overall HEMP plan) that lists units with RAM Red or Yellow 5A/5B process safety risks, the units to be reviewed each year, the selected HAZOP approach per unit, and suitable scheduling, budgeting, and resource allocation to meet the 5-year revalidation requirement.
csfWho: HEMP Process Owner with support from HEMP Focal Point / HEMP Coordinator; Asset leadership
csfWhat: Establish and maintain an Asset HAZOP plan covering unit listing, yearly review schedule, approach per unit, and resourcing
csfWhen: On an ongoing basis as part of HEMP planning; reviewed at least yearly by leadership
csfWhere: At the Asset / site level, within the HEMP plan
csfWhy: To ensure systematic, scheduled hazard analysis of units with RAM Red/Yellow 5A/5B process safety risks and to maintain 5-year compliance
csfHow: Compile a list of units with RAM Red/Yellow 5A/5B risks; assign review year and approach (new/redo/update); schedule key dates; secure budget and competent resources; account for PSI status and additional studies (OMAR, LOPA, relief valve revalidation)
csfEvidence: Asset HAZOP plan / HEMP plan (Appendix A template), schedule, budget allocation records
keyActivities: List units with RAM Red/Yellow 5A/5B process safety risks | Assign review year and HAZOP approach per unit | Schedule planning, preparation and workshop dates | Confirm PSI and specialised study status | Allocate budget and competent resources
riskAddressed: Overdue or unplanned process hazard analyses leading to undetected process safety risks and non-compliance with the 5-year revalidation requirement
testingApproach: Document review of HAZOP/HEMP plan | Verify schedule covers all in-scope units | Check resource and budget allocation records

controlStatement: The Asset must complete the next HAZOP study workshop within 5 years of completion of the previous HAZOP workshop for each unit/Asset, and must issue the final HAZOP report within 90 days of the workshop.
csfWho: HEMP Process Owner / HEMP Focal Point / HEMP Coordinator
csfWhat: Ensure HAZOP workshops are completed within the 5-year cycle and final reports issued within 90 days
csfWhen: 5-year cycle starts at completion of the previous HAZOP workshop (not at management approval or startup); report within 90 days of workshop
csfWhy: To maintain valid, current hazard analyses and meet the AIPSM 5-Year Review requirement
csfHow: Track previous workshop completion dates; schedule and conduct the next workshop before the 5-year deadline; issue the final report within 90 days
csfEvidence: HAZOP workshop completion records, final HAZOP reports with issue dates, overdue PHA KPI
keyActivities: Record HAZOP workshop completion dates | Schedule next workshop within 5-year window | Conduct workshop ahead of deadline | Issue final report within 90 days
riskAddressed: Expired/invalid hazard analyses; process safety risks not reassessed against current conditions
testingApproach: Audit of HAZOP completion dates against 5-year cycle | Verify report issue dates within 90 days | Review overdue PHA metric

controlName: Designation of HEMP Roles and Competent HAZOP Team
controlStatement: Each Asset must designate a HEMP Process Owner and a HEMP Focal Point/Coordinator with defined accountabilities, and must assure that the HAZOP team and facilitators have the competency required by the HAZOP DEP.
csfWho: Asset leadership (designation); HEMP Process Owner (competency assurance); HEMP Focal Point/Coordinator (maintains approved facilitator list)
csfWhat: Assign HEMP roles and ensure HAZOP team/facilitator competency
csfWhen: During planning of the 5-year HAZOP studies and on an ongoing basis
csfWhy: Quality of the PHA is highly dependent on team competency; clear accountability supports effective execution
csfHow: Designate HEMP Process Owner and Focal Point/Coordinator; maintain an approved list of HAZOP facilitators/chairs; verify competency against DEP 80.00.00.15 requirements
csfEvidence: Role assignment records, approved facilitator list, competency/qualification records
keyActivities: Designate HEMP Process Owner and Focal Point/Coordinator | Maintain approved facilitator/chair list | Verify team competency per HAZOP DEP | Document accountabilities
riskAddressed: Poor-quality hazard analysis resulting from inadequate competency or unclear accountability
testingApproach: Review of role designations | Verify facilitator approval list | Check competency records against DEP
